selected.tweet_text:
THIS GUY SHOWED THE CODEX SUBAGENT FLOW FOR FINISHING APPS 20X FASTER

The useful part is not “AI writes code.”

It is the shape of the prompt.

Most people use Codex like this:

one chat,
one task,
one thread,
then you personally remember the UI bugs, product logic, copy, assets, routes, and launch problems.

That is still you operating the project.

The video shows a cleaner pattern:

make Codex the operator.

The user becomes the president.
The main Codex thread becomes the VP.
The subagents become employees.

The flow is simple:

ask Codex to open multiple threads,
have each one inspect a different part of the app,
put the main agent in charge,
open the dev server,
let the subagents report back,
then have the main thread synthesize the product.

That is the part builders should steal.

Not the exact wording.
The org chart.

One subagent can look at UI states.
One can check backend logic.
One can clean copy.
One can inspect assets.
One can look at the launch path.

The win is fewer context collisions.

Instead of forcing one chat to hold the entire app in its head forever, you split the app into smaller jobs and make the main agent merge the results.

Caveat:

this does not replace taste.

If the spec is vague, you just get vague work in parallel.

But if you already know what the app needs to become, this is the move from vibe coding to something closer to managing a tiny project team inside Codex.
